description: Swagger Petstore sample API for managing pets, stores, and users.

endpoints.description: Updates an existing pet by Id. Requires a full Pet object in the request body. Returns the updated Pet object. Edge cases include invalid or missing fields, nonexistent pets, or validation errors.

request_body.schema_summary: Pet object with id, name, photoUrls, category, tags, and status.

endpoints.description: Creates a new pet in the store. Requires a Pet object in the request body. Edge cases include missing required fields or invalid structure.

request_body.schema_summary: Pet object with name and photoUrls required.

endpoints.description: Returns pets filtered by status. Multiple comma-separated statuses allowed.
Returns pets filtered by tags. Query accepts comma-separated list.
Returns a single pet. Supports authentication via api_key or OAuth2.

parameters.name: petId
parameters.description: ID of the pet to return.

endpoints.summary: Update a pet with form data.
endpoints.description: Updates specific fields of a pet using form data via query parameters.
Deletes a pet by ID. Optional api_key header may be used.
Uploads an image file for a pet. Accepts binary data in request body.
Returns inventory counts by status.
Creates an order for a pet.

request_body.schema_summary: Order object containing id, petId, quantity, shipDate, status, complete.

endpoints.description: Returns order if ID <= 5 or > 10. Other values return errors.
Deletes order with ID < 1000.
Creates a user. Requires a full User object.

request_body.schema_summary: User object with id, username, firstName, lastName, email, password, phone, userStatus.

endpoints.description: Creates users from an input array.
Logs user in. Returns a session token string.
Logs out current user.
Returns user details by username.
Updates a user by username.

request_body.schema_summary: User object with fields to update.

endpoints.description: Deletes a user by username.

description: Petstore API for managing pets, store orders, inventory, and users.

endpoints.description: Update an existing pet by Id using a full Pet object. The entire pet resource is replaced with the provided representation, so omitted optional fields may be cleared (inferred). The ID in the body must refer to an existing pet; otherwise a 404 is returned. Validation errors on required fields (e.g., name, photoUrls) or invalid enum values for status produce a 422. A 400 indicates an invalid ID or structurally incorrect payload. The operation requires OAuth2 petstore_auth with write:pets and read:pets scopes.

request_body.schema_summary: Pet object with id, name, category, photoUrls, tags, and status.

200.description: Successful operation; returns the updated Pet resource.
200.schema_summary: Pet object representing the updated pet.

400.description: Invalid ID supplied or malformed Pet payload.

example.detail: id must be a positive integer (int64).

404.description: Pet not found with the given ID.

example.detail: No pet exists with id=10.

422.description: Validation exception for required fields or enum constraints.

example.detail: Field 'name' is required.
An unexpected error occurred while updating the pet.

endpoints.summary: Add a new pet to the store.
endpoints.description: Create a new pet in the store using a Pet object. The server may generate the pet ID if not provided (inferred). Required fields include name and photoUrls; missing required fields or invalid enum values for status cause a 422. A 400 indicates a structurally invalid payload. On success, the persisted Pet is returned. Requires OAuth2 petstore_auth with write:pets and read:pets scopes.

request_body.schema_summary: Pet object with at least name and photoUrls; optional id, category, tags, and status.

200.description: Successful operation; returns the newly created Pet.
200.schema_summary: Pet object representing the created pet, including its assigned id.

400.description: Invalid input; request body is syntactically incorrect or cannot be parsed.

example.detail: Request body is not valid JSON.

422.description: Validation exception; missing required fields or invalid values.

example.detail: Field 'photoUrls' is required.
An unexpected error occurred while adding the pet.

endpoints.description: Retrieve a list of pets filtered by their status. Multiple status values can be provided as a comma-separated list in a single status parameter due to explode=true on a string (inferred). Valid status values are available, pending, and sold. If no explicit value is provided, the default is available. A 400 is returned for invalid status values. Requires OAuth2 petstore_auth with write:pets and read:pets scopes.

parameters.name: status
parameters.description: Status values to filter pets by; can be a single value or a comma-separated list of available, pending, sold.

200.description: successful operation; returns an array of Pet objects matching the requested status values.

400.description: Invalid status value; one or more provided statuses are not in the allowed set.

example.detail: status must be one of: available, pending, sold.
An unexpected error occurred while searching for pets.

endpoints.description: Retrieve a list of pets filtered by one or more tags. Multiple tags can be provided either as repeated query parameters or as a comma-separated string depending on client behavior; the API expects an array type (inferred). A 400 is returned for invalid tag values or malformed array input. Requires OAuth2 petstore_auth with write:pets and read:pets scopes.

parameters.name: tags
parameters.description: Tags used to filter pets. Use tag1, tag2, tag3 for testing.

200.description: successful operation; returns an array of Pet objects that have any of the specified tags.

example.detail: One or more tags contain invalid characters.
An unexpected error occurred while searching for pets.

endpoints.description: Retrieve a single pet by its unique identifier. A 400 is returned if the ID is not a valid int64, and 404 if no pet exists with the given ID. This endpoint supports either API key auth via the api_key header or OAuth2 petstore_auth with write:pets and read:pets scopes.

parameters.name: petId
parameters.description: ID of the pet to return.

200.description: successful operation; returns the Pet with the specified ID.

400.description: Invalid ID supplied; not a valid int64.

example.detail: petId must be a positive integer (int64).
No pet exists with id=10.
An unexpected error occurred while retrieving the pet.

endpoints.summary: Updates a pet in the store with form data.
endpoints.description: Update an existing pet's basic fields (name and status) using form-like parameters. Only the provided fields are updated (inferred). A 400 is returned for invalid input, such as a non-integer petId or invalid status value. On success, a representation of the updated Pet is returned. Requires OAuth2 petstore_auth with write:pets and read:pets scopes.

parameters.name: petId
parameters.description: ID of the pet that needs to be updated.

200.description: successful operation; returns the updated Pet.

400.description: Invalid input; invalid petId or invalid query parameter values.

example.detail: status must be one of: available, pending, sold.
An unexpected error occurred while updating the pet.

endpoints.description: Delete a pet by its ID. If api_key is provided in the header, it may be used for authorization in addition to OAuth2 scopes (inferred). A 400 is returned if the petId is invalid; 200 indicates successful deletion even if the response body is empty. A 404 may be returned via the default error when the pet does not exist (inferred). Requires OAuth2 petstore_auth with write:pets and read:pets scopes.

parameters.name: petId
parameters.description: ID of the pet to delete.

200.description: Pet deleted; the resource is no longer available.
200.schema_summary: May return a confirmation object or be empty (inferred).

400.description: Invalid pet value; petId is invalid.

example.detail: petId must be a positive integer (int64).
An unexpected error occurred while deleting the pet.

endpoints.description: Upload an image file associated with a specific pet. The file is sent as raw binary in the request body with content type application/octet-stream. Optional additionalMetadata can be provided as a query parameter. A 400 is returned if no file is uploaded; 404 if the pet does not exist. On success, an ApiResponse containing a status code, type, and message is returned. Requires OAuth2 petstore_auth with write:pets and read:pets scopes.

parameters.name: petId
parameters.description: ID of the pet to associate the uploaded image with.

parameters.name: additionalMetadata
parameters.description: Additional metadata to store with the image.

request_body.schema_summary: Binary image data (e.g., JPEG, PNG) for the pet.

200.description: successful operation; image accepted and processed.
200.schema_summary: ApiResponse with code, type, and message summarizing the upload result.

400.description: No file uploaded or invalid file.

example.detail: Request body is empty or not binary content.
No pet exists with id=10.
An unexpected error occurred while uploading the image.

endpoints.description: Return a map of inventory counts keyed by pet status. Typical keys include available, pending, and sold (inferred). Each value is an integer quantity. Authentication via the api_key header is required. The response is suitable for dashboards or monitoring inventory levels.

200.schema_summary: Object whose keys are status strings and values are int32 quantities.

example.detail: An unexpected error occurred while retrieving inventory.

endpoints.summary: Place an order for a pet.
endpoints.description: Create a new purchase order for a pet. The Order object may include petId, quantity, shipDate, status, and complete flag. A 400 is returned for malformed input; 422 for validation issues such as invalid status enum or missing required fields (inferred). On success, the created Order is returned.

request_body.schema_summary: Order object with optional id, petId, quantity, shipDate, status, and complete.

200.description: successful operation; returns the created Order.
200.schema_summary: Order object including its assigned id.

400.description: Invalid input; malformed JSON or logically invalid order data.

example.detail: quantity must be a positive integer.

422.description: Validation exception; order does not meet schema constraints.

example.detail: status must be one of: placed, approved, delivered.
An unexpected error occurred while placing the order.

endpoints.description: Retrieve a purchase order by its ID. For testing, valid responses are typically returned when orderId is an integer <= 5 or > 10; other values may generate exceptions as per the sample implementation. A 400 is returned for invalid IDs (non-integer or out of acceptable range), and 404 when the order is not found.

parameters.name: orderId
parameters.description: ID of the order to fetch.

200.description: successful operation; returns the Order with the specified ID.

400.description: Invalid ID supplied; not a valid int64 or outside acceptable demo range.

example.detail: orderId must be a positive integer (int64).
No order exists with id=10.
An unexpected error occurred while retrieving the order.

endpoints.description: Delete a purchase order by its ID. For demo purposes, valid deletions typically occur for IDs < 1000; larger or non-integer values generate API errors. A 400 is returned for invalid IDs; 404 if the order does not exist. A 200 indicates the order has been deleted.

parameters.name: orderId
parameters.description: ID of the order to delete.

200.schema_summary: May return a confirmation object or be empty (inferred).

example.detail: orderId must be an integer less than 1000.
No order exists with id=55.
An unexpected error occurred while deleting the order.

endpoints.description: Create a new user. This operation is intended to be performed by an already authenticated user with sufficient privileges (inferred). The request body must contain a User object. On success, the created User representation is returned. Validation or persistence errors are returned via the default error response (inferred).

request_body.schema_summary: User object with id, username, firstName, lastName, email, password, phone, and userStatus.

200.description: successful operation; returns the created User.

example.detail: An unexpected error occurred while creating the user.

endpoints.summary: Creates list of users with given input array.
endpoints.description: Bulk create users from an array of User objects. On success, a representation of a single User (typically the last or a summary user in this demo API) is returned. The default response handles any unexpected errors, including partial failures (inferred).

request_body.schema_summary: Array of User objects to be created.

200.description: Successful operation; returns a User object (demo behavior).
200.schema_summary: User object, often echoing one of the created users (demo behavior, inferred).

example.detail: An unexpected error occurred while creating users.

endpoints.description: Log a user into the system using username and password query parameters. On success, returns a token string or a session identifier (demo behavior) along with rate-limiting headers X-Rate-Limit and X-Expires-After. A 400 is returned for invalid credentials or missing parameters.

parameters.name: password
parameters.description: The password for login in clear text.

200.description: successful operation; returns a token or session identifier as a string.
200.schema_summary: String token, with X-Rate-Limit and X-Expires-After headers indicating quota and expiry.

example.detail: Username or password is incorrect.
An unexpected error occurred while logging in.

endpoints.summary: Logs out current logged in user session.
endpoints.description: Log out the current user, invalidating their session or token (demo behavior). Typically requires that the request is authenticated, but the specific mechanism is implementation-dependent (inferred). A 200 indicates the session was successfully terminated.

200.description: successful operation; user is logged out.

example.detail: An unexpected error occurred while logging out.

endpoints.description: Retrieve user details for the given username. For testing, username user1 is commonly available. A 400 is returned for invalid usernames (e.g., empty or malformed), and 404 if the user is not found.

parameters.name: username
parameters.description: The username to fetch. Use user1 for testing.

example.detail: username must be a non-empty string.
No user exists with username 'user1'.
An unexpected error occurred while retrieving the user.

endpoints.description: Update an existing user identified by username. The request body contains a full User object representing the new state. This operation is restricted to logged-in users with the appropriate permissions (inferred). A 400 indicates a bad request (e.g., invalid body); 404 indicates that the user does not exist.

parameters.name: username
parameters.description: Username of the user to update.

200.schema_summary: May return no body or a confirmation message (inferred).

400.description: bad request; invalid payload or parameters.

example.detail: Request body is not valid JSON.
No user exists with username 'user1'.
An unexpected error occurred while updating the user.

endpoints.description: Delete a user by username. This can only be done by a logged-in user with sufficient privileges (inferred). A 400 is returned for invalid usernames, and 404 if the user does not exist. A 200 indicates successful deletion.

parameters.name: username
parameters.description: The username of the user to delete.

example.detail: username must be a non-empty string.
No user exists with username 'user1'.
An unexpected error occurred while deleting the user.
